text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously risk-on. The real driver for your names is headline 4 - China factory activity beating on tech export demand. That's a direct tailwind for semis and chip names. Iran-strike-Monday-rally pattern (headline 8) adds a geopolitical bid under SPY/QQQ. Yen at a 40-year low keeps macro volatility simmering in the background.

Your holdings: SOXL (-7.06%) is the one needing attention - it's a 3x leveraged semiconductor ETF, so every bad day hits triple. Stop is $200, price is $236, that's still a $36 cushion but leverage decay is real. SPCX is brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history - on margin this is genuinely dangerous if it gaps down. SMH (-0.69%) is minor but watch it as your semiconductor barometer.

Watch: 1) Whether China tech export demand headline actually lifts SMH and SOXL today. 2) SOXL holding above $220 intraday - if it cracks, your stop at $200 gets tested fast on margin.

Bottom line: Your biggest risk today is SOXL leverage decay meeting any macro wobble - watch that level hard.
plays: SOXL: Stop is $200 - consider tightening it to $220 given 3x decay risk and your margin exposure. No reason to let a -7% position become -15%.
SPCX: Size must stay tiny. Brand-new IPO, no data, margin amplifies any gap-down. If you're uncomfortable, trim to a size where a 20% drop doesn't sting.
No new buys today - China export headline is encouraging for semis but not enough of a catalyst to chase ALAB or AMAT at premium prices on margin. Hold and watch SMH for confirmation.
